extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked with 2.5km to go on the final Col de Turini climb, dropped VINGEGAARD briefly before regrouping occurred in closing meters.
tactical_patterns: FOSS paced entire field for UAE until 6km out on 15km climb, revealing UAE sacrificed depth for tempo control.
POGAČAR attacked twice on upper slopes when VINGEGAARD already struggling, exploiting hesitation after first catch rather than distancing alone.
Peloton looked around in final 300m allowing YATES, POELS, MÄDER, SIVAKOV to regain 53 seconds—fatal tactical error before final stage.
JORGENSON dropped before final 4km despite Jumbo support, signaling vulnerability on sustained gradients despite podium position.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Won after being caught once, demonstrating repeatable acceleration on 15km climbs when fresher than stage 5.
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: Limited time loss compared to stage 5 despite similar tactic from POGAČAR, suggesting recovery or tactical learning.
rider_signals.FOSS Tobias: positive: Solo paced for 9km on final climb as last UAE helper, elite diesel capacity at threshold.
rider_signals.GAUDU David: neutral: Finished second but gained time only through peloton hesitation, no independent attacking threat shown.
rider_signals.JORGENSON Matteo: negative: Dropped before final 4km on 15km climb despite team support, concerning for stage race contention.
rider_signals.MARTÍNEZ Daniel Felipe: negative: Dropped early when FOSS began pacing at 15km out, lacks resistance to sustained tempo.
rider_signals.YATES Simon: positive: Gained back 53 seconds in final sprint after being distanced, opportunistic positioning salvaged GC damage.
